text.title: Creational Design Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every creational-patterns application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Default to Dependency Injection for any component with dependencies. Never reach for Singleton unless the dependency is truly process-global, read-only, and stateless.
rule.rationale: DI reduces maintenance bugs by ~27%, improves testability (70% of high-performing teams rely on mocking), enables modularity, and accelerates refactoring. DI frameworks exist in every mainstream language.

rule.statement: Use Builder when a constructor has more than 3 parameters or any optional parameter, instead of overloading constructors or passing positional `None`/`null` placeholders.
rule.rationale: Telescoping constructors are unreadable and break call-site refactoring. Builders make required vs optional explicit and produce immutable results.

rule.statement: Use Factory Method when the concrete subclass to instantiate depends on runtime input (config, env, request payload). Do not litter `if/elif` constructor switches across business logic.
rule.rationale: Centralizing the switch in one factory keeps domain code closed against new variants and makes it possible to add a new variant by registering it once.

rule.statement: Adoption of any creational pattern MUST be justified by a construction-hotspot record (location + reason). Patterns without a hotspot are blocked.
rule.rationale: Pattern overhead is justified by construction-complexity removal; without that the pattern is pure indirection.

rule.statement: Singleton MUST NOT be the default choice. Singletons need a documented invariant that genuinely requires a single instance.
rule.rationale: Singletons hide shared mutable state and break test isolation; the default is module/request/app-scoped DI.

rule.statement: Builder pattern adopted only when the target type has ≥4 construction params OR ≥1 invariant validated mid-construction.
rule.rationale: Below the threshold, plain dataclass with defaults is simpler and equally clear.

rule.statement: Factory Method / Abstract Factory adopted only when ≥2 concrete products exist or are imminent. Single-product factories are blocked.
rule.rationale: Single-product factory is indirection without variance; remove until a second variant appears.

rule.statement: Every DI registration MUST declare its scope (request / app / singleton / transient). Implicit defaults are blocked.
rule.rationale: Scope ambiguity is the leading cause of subtle leak / shared-state bugs in DI-heavy codebases.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
